svg.title: Pixel Eruv — Browser Architecture (Client-Side)
svg.desc: The browser-side architecture: Phaser 4 renderer with GPU layers, LiveKit JS SDK for audio/video, WebSocket transport for game state, replication decoder, local entity store, netcode (prediction + interpolation), input capture, interaction system, extension component renderers, and the external services they connect to (Dex, SeaweedFS, Pusher, LiveKit SFU).
svg.text: Phaser 4 renderer · LiveKit JS SDK · WebSocket transport · replication decoder · netcode · input · extensions

g.text: • Redirect to Dex → OIDC login → JWT
• Load Phaser app bundle · load map/tileset URLs from PocketBase config
• Fetch Tiled JSON + tileset images from SeaweedFS
• Cache in browser · feed to Phaser TilemapGPULayer
• Publish local mic/camera · subscribe to remote tracks
• DestroyEntity → remove entity · PlayAnimation → trigger VFX
• Client-side prediction for local avatar (instant feel)
• Server reconciliation (replay un-acked inputs by seq)
• Dead reckoning on packet loss · snapshot_seq gap detection
Mirrors the server's replicated ECS state — component-based, not entity-type-based
Entities = {entity_id → {component_id → component_data}} · Generic: a Position update is handled identically for players, NPCs, or objects
Local avatar's Position is driven by prediction, not by UpdateComponent (other components still replicated)
• Keyboard / touch → InputState (up, down, left, right, run)
• Fed to prediction (local) + sent to Pusher (authoritative)
• Popup UI for interactable objects (actions from components)
• Knock-to-join · sit/sleep · open URL · talk to NPC
chat · knock popup · interaction menu · status
